data.body:
MEASUREMENT FRAMEWORK (AC2) — recorded at merge time, 2026-07-12. This epic closes on the measurement FRAMEWORK being in place; the actual multi-day post-deployment telemetry is DOCUMENTED here + in the committed docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md, and is picked up naturally by the NEXT calibration session (no standalone tracking ticket — operator decision 2026-07-12; the interim idea ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d was closed as handled-by-process).

── (a) PRE-CHANGE BASELINE (from the 2026-07-12 calibration analysis: session logs remarkable-hydrated-nightjar / tainted-continuing-queensnake / shaggy-linoleum-gavial) ──
- Code-review blocking: **0 "BLOCK — finding" votes across 162 post-recalibration changes.** No real finding ever reached the 0.95 threshold: change #529's untested-degradation advisory scored impact 0.0 (went red on main 57 min after merge); #518's importlib code-execution findings ~0.54; #539's correctness findings ~0.60.
- Plan-review advisory load: **~13 advisories/round**, with ~95% of remediation edits minting new findings (protocol-inflated by the CLAUDE.md "remediate ALL valid advisories" mandate, since loosened by e1f2).

── (b) EXACT RE-RUNNABLE POST-CHANGE QUERIES ──
Sidecar layout (both gates): each REVIEW_RESULT event file is top-level {event_type, data, timestamp, uuid, author, env_id}; the PAYLOAD is event["data"] (NOT "payload"). Code-review payloads carry schema="code_review_result_v1", impact_model_version ("code-v2" pre-f32e, "code-v3" after) and blocking/advisory finding arrays with per-finding priority. Plan-review payloads carry schema="plan_review_result_v1" with findings[] carrying .criteria, .decision, .validity, and a stable .norm_id.

1) Code-review block / false-block counts over the code-v2 cohort (the cohort b9c0's 0.54 threshold governs — code-v3 is a fresh cohort, out of scope here):
   # blocks: findings decided "block" in code-v2 sidecars
   find .tickets-tracker -name '*REVIEW_RESULT.json' -print0 \
     | xargs -0 -I{} jq -c 'select(.data.schema=="code_review_result_v1" and .data.impact_model_version=="code-v2")
         | .data.findings[]? | select(.decision=="block") | {ticket:.criteria, priority}' {} \
     | wc -l
   # A "false block" is a block later reverted / thumbed-down on main — reconcile the blocked
   # change-ids against the post-merge revert/red set from the field-telemetry pull (deferred, 72b6).

2) Plan-review advisory UPTAKE via norm_id survival across rounds (did agents organically act on advisories once the mandate was loosened, or do the same norm_ids persist round-over-round?):
   # per ticket, collect the sequence of finding norm_ids per review round, newest-first:
   find .tickets-tracker -name '*REVIEW_RESULT.json' -print0 \
     | xargs -0 -I{} jq -c 'select(.data.schema=="plan_review_result_v1")
         | {ts:.timestamp, ticket:(.data.ticket_id // input_filename),
            norms:[.data.findings[]? | select(.decision=="surfaced") | .norm_id]}' {} \
     | sort
   # A norm_id that DISAPPEARS between an earlier and later round for the same ticket = an advisory
   # the author acted on (organic uptake); a norm_id that SURVIVES unaddressed = ignored advisory.
   # Track the survival rate and the advisories-per-round count; compare against the ~13/round baseline.

Also observe G7 (d4cf) fire-rate from day one — the exact jq query + the 0.6-0.7 promotion band are the committed, CI-verifiable artifact docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md.

── (c) DEFERRED FIELD DATA (documented, not ticket-tracked) ──
The ACTUAL post-deployment field data (multi-day block/false-block counts + advisory-uptake rates after these changes ship) is OUT of this epic's close path. It is not tracked as a standalone ticket: the queries above + docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/g7-promotion-path.md ARE the durable logging spec, and the next calibration session re-runs them against the post-deploy cohort (as this 2026-07-12 session did for the prior cohort). Operator decision 2026-07-12: the interim idea ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d was closed as handled-by-process.

CAVEAT on b9c0's 0.54 threshold: PROVISIONAL. b9c0's AC records that a held-out (non-circular) adjudication must confirm it before the code-review block is relied on in production; the first-pass derivation used a circular proxy.
